content.text: Options for trapping Tab focus inside a container.
Element that receives focus when the trap activates.
Restore focus to the previously focused element when the trap releases.
Whether the focus trap is active.
Per-document trap stacks: only the top entry captures focus.
Splice an incoming ancestor before its existing descendant so the descendant
stays on top; otherwise activation order makes the new trap the top.
* Keeps Tab and Shift+Tab focus inside a container while active, with nested
* trap stacking and optional focus restoration on release. Active traps listen
* for keydown and focusin in the capture phase on the container's owner document.
Detach listeners BEFORE focus moves out in release(), or the document-level
focusin recapture re-traps the restored target. restoreOnUnmount:false keeps
this stack hook from moving focus during its own unmount cleanup.
No dependency array on purpose: React does not re-fire effects when
containerRef.current mutates while the ref object stays stable.
restoreFocus is release-time policy; update in place instead of tearing
down (which would recapture the interior as the restore target, not the opener).
Capture the opener before focus moves inside, so a false-to-true toggle can still restore to it.
Push BEFORE arming/focusing: pushTrap suspends the previous top so its
focusin handler does not recapture focus away from this new trap.
Detach own listeners BEFORE removeTrap resumes the outer trap; otherwise
our focusin sees the outer trap's refocus as an escape and fights it.
Options for trapping Tab focus inside a container.
Element that receives focus when the trap activates.
Restore focus to the previously focused element when the trap releases.
Whether the focus trap is active.
Per-document trap stacks: only the top entry captures focus.
Splice an incoming ancestor before its existing descendant so the descendant
stays on top; otherwise activation order makes the new trap the top.
* Keeps Tab and Shift+Tab focus inside a container while active, with nested
* trap stacking and optional focus restoration on release. Active traps listen
* for keydown and focusin in the capture phase on the container's owner document.
Detach listeners BEFORE focus moves out in release(), or the document-level
focusin recapture re-traps the restored target. restoreOnUnmount:false keeps
this stack hook from moving focus during its own unmount cleanup.
No dependency array on purpose: React does not re-fire effects when
containerRef.current mutates while the ref object stays stable.
restoreFocus is release-time policy; update in place instead of tearing
down (which would recapture the interior as the restore target, not the opener).
Capture the opener before focus moves inside, so a false-to-true toggle can still restore to it.
Push BEFORE arming/focusing: pushTrap suspends the previous top so its
focusin handler does not recapture focus away from this new trap.
Detach own listeners BEFORE removeTrap resumes the outer trap; otherwise
our focusin sees the outer trap's refocus as an escape and fights it.
Options for capturing and restoring focus around temporary UI.
Whether capture and restore are active.
Restore focus during cleanup if capture was called and restore was not called manually.
Fallback element to focus when the captured target is unavailable.
Stores the current focus target for the provided document.
Focuses the captured or fallback target and returns whether focus moved.
The last captured focus target, or null when nothing is captured.
* Captures the current focus target and restores it later, with per-document
Options shared by the imperative focus-restore utilities.
Passes `preventScroll` to the focus call when restoring focus.
* Returns the current restorable focus target, ignoring body, documentElement,
* Focuses a connected target and returns whether the document's active element
Older engines / some jsdom versions reject the FocusOptions argument; fall back to plain focus().
Per ARIA, aria-hidden="false" does NOT re-expose content hidden by an aria-hidden="true" ancestor.
* Returns false when a hidden, inert, or aria-hidden="true" self-or-ancestor
* (across shadow boundaries) removes the element from keyboard reach. Shared
* with navigation discovery so it skips the same unreachable items focus does.
Descendants of the first <legend> are not disabled per spec; keep searching upward.
* Returns true for visible, non-disabled elements that can receive programmatic
jsdom returns compound-selector matches out of tree order; normalize.
Sort comparator that returns elements in composed order across open shadow roots.
* Returns all focusable descendants in DOM order, including programmatic focus
* targets that are not reachable by Tab.
* Returns browser Tab-order descendants, excluding negative tabindex targets
* and collapsing native radio groups to one Tab stop.
Returns the first focusable descendant in DOM order.
Returns true when the element contains its owner document's deep active element, across shadow roots.
Returns the `Window` realm for a DOM value, or null for non-DOM values.
Realm-safe HTMLElement guard for elements from iframes or other documents.
Realm-safe Node guard using a known owner window.
Returns the shadow host when a node lives in a shadow root, else null.
Returns the deepest active element, descending through open shadow roots.
Returns true when `container` contains `target` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
Returns the nearest self-or-ancestor matching `selector` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
* Returns the deepest composed event target, past the retargeted shadow host.
* Returns true for form-like or editable elements that can own keyboard input,
* including select and non-text input types.
* Returns true only for enabled text-editable targets that should keep typing
